summary: The functional test `test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub]` failed after 3 retries because GitHub's servers returned repeated 502 errors when pip tried to download `https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip`. This is a transient network/upstream failure, not a code defect.
root_cause: GitHub returned repeated HTTP 502 responses when the test attempted to download pip-test-package from GitHub, causing pip to exhaust its retries and exit with code 1. The error was: 'HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))'

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: This is the actual error message from pip, showing that GitHub returned 502 errors repeatedly, causing the network request to fail.
The test was retried 3 times (via pytest-rerunfailures), and failed all 3 times with the same 502 network error, confirming it is a transient network issue.
The final test result shows the test failed with a non-zero exit code from pip, triggered by the network failure.
pip exited with code 1 due to the network failure when trying to fetch the package from GitHub.

suggested_fix: Re-run the CI job; this is a transient GitHub 502 network failure. If it recurs frequently, consider adding retry logic at the test level or mocking the GitHub download in the test.
